articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices. This innovation enhances user experience by allowing complex actions like calendar management and game logic execution without internet dependency.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances mobile AI capabilities, making complex tasks accessible offline.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem

articles.why_it_matters: Expands the potential of AI agents by integrating with popular development tools.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event will cover topics like Gemini multimodal breakthroughs and zero-trust security, emphasizing the importance of in-person collaboration.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the value of in-person events for networking and learning in tech.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers faster GPU and NPU acceleration and improved support for PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI and on-device inference.
articles.why_it_matters: Sets a new standard for performance in AI deployment frameworks.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through interactive, hands-on building. The program covers advanced topics and provides immediate, practical results for participants.
articles.why_it_matters: Encourages active learning and skill development in cloud technologies.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience

These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.

Now, no system is perfect, but it turns […]

articles.ai_summary: Mbodi AI, a robotics startup from Y Combinator, is looking to hire talent to advance its embodied AI platform, which aims to enable robots to learn and operate like humans. This reflects the growing interest in AI robotics.
articles.why_it_matters: Demonstrates the expanding job market in AI robotics and innovation.

articles.title: Do less with AI
articles.snippet: The promises of automation can only come true if you’re working smarter, not harder
articles.ai_summary: The article discusses how effective automation requires smarter work practices rather than simply doing less, emphasizing strategic implementation.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the importance of intelligent automation strategies in AI adoption.

articles.title: The cofounder shakeup at xAI is vintage Elon Musk
articles.snippet: xAI lost Ross Nordeen, its final non-Musk cofounder, this month. What does the turnover mean for the startup's looming IPO with SpaceX?
articles.ai_summary: xAI experienced a cofounder shakeup with the departure of Ross Nordeen, raising questions about the startup's future and its IPO plans with SpaceX.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals potential instability at xAI, impacting investor confidence ahead of its IPO.

articles.title: Apple’s Biggest Shift Ever? iPhone Fold Rumored to Debut Alongside iPhone 18 Pro this September
articles.snippet: Apple is reportedly preparing to unveil its first foldable iPhone, tentatively named the “iPhone Fold,” as part of the anticipated iPhone 18 Pro lineup. This new device is expected to debut in September 2026, a date that is part of Apple’s 50th anniversary. T…
articles.ai_summary: Rumors suggest that Apple will launch its first foldable iPhone, the 'iPhone Fold,' alongside the iPhone 18 Pro in September 2026, coinciding with its 50th anniversary.
articles.why_it_matters: Could redefine the smartphone market and Apple's product strategy.

articles.title: ETtech Deals Digest: Startups raise $187 million this week, down 28% on-year
articles.snippet: The number of deals during the week was also down at 18 transactions, compared to 24 in the corresponding week last year. Compared to the previous week, when startups raised $310.7 million, the funding was down 39.8%.
articles.ai_summary: Startups raised $187 million this week, a 28% decrease compared to last year, with fewer deals indicating a slowdown in funding activity.
articles.why_it_matters: Reflects a cooling trend in startup funding, which could affect innovation.

articles.title: AI is rewiring Bollywood, the world's most prolific film industry
articles.snippet: AI is transforming Bollywood by enhancing filmmaking efficiency and creating innovative content amid changing audience preferences.
articles.ai_summary: AI is significantly impacting Bollywood by streamlining production processes and fostering new creative content in response to evolving audience tastes.
articles.why_it_matters: Demonstrates AI's transformative role in creative industries like film.
